name: Haiti
avoid: Haiti has a severe security situation and many governments advise against travel; if travel is essential, keep plans flexible, move only in daylight, use hotel-arranged airport transfers, and rely on vetted local guides and drivers.
April to October is wetter and hotter, while August to October brings the highest hurricane disruption risk for domestic flights, coastal boats, and mountain roads.
Intercity routes can close with little notice because of protests, roadblocks, fuel shortages, or security incidents, so do not stack tight same-day connections.
stopoverNote: Port-au-Prince remains the main coordination hub for most Haiti trips because Sunrise Airways flights, vetted private drivers, and secure hotel pickups usually route through the capital before onward travel.

cities.name: Port-au-Prince
cities.notes: Capital base for museums, markets, galleries, rum, and the easiest place to organize vetted transfers, secure hotels, and onward flights or drivers.

cities.name: Cap-Haïtien
cities.notes: Best northern base for Cap street life, boulevard cafés, and the Milot day trip to Sans-Souci Palace and Citadelle Laferrière.

cities.name: Jacmel
cities.notes: Haiti’s artsy south-coast city for gingerbread-era streets, boardwalk sunsets, beach stops, and the jungle pools of Bassin Bleu.

cities.name: Île-à-Vache
cities.notes: Slow island add-on off Les Cayes for boat hops, fishing villages, and long beach time once you have confirmed weather, boat timing, and accommodation.

itinerary.sim: Digicel or Natcom prepaid data usually works out to about ₹900–₹1,900 for a short-stay pack. Coverage is best in Port-au-Prince and Cap-Haïtien; download offline maps before Jacmel waterfalls, Milot hill climbs, and Île-à-Vache boat transfers.

Port-au-Prince.name: Port-au-Prince
Port-au-Prince.note: Stay in Pétion-Ville, Turgeau, or a major business-hotel zone, line up all airport and city transfers in advance, and avoid independent night movement.

activities.name: Musée du Panthéon National Haïtien (MUPANAH) admission
Marché en Fer (Iron Market) walk with local guide or driver drop-off
Champ de Mars and Cathédrale Notre-Dame ruins stop
Barbancourt Rum Distillery tour or tasting

days.theme: Fort Jacques, Art Stops & Pétion-Ville Dinner

activities.name: Fort Jacques admission
Observatoire de Boutilliers viewpoint stop
Atis Resistance or El-Saieh Gallery visit
Evening coffee or dessert run in Pétion-Ville

days.theme: Museums, Crafts & a Lighter Capital Day

activities.name: Bureau of Ethnology visit
Papillon Enterprise artisan workshop stop
Hotel Montana view stop or coffee
Driver-led shopping buffer for crafts or supplies

Cap-Haïtien.name: Cap-Haïtien
Cap-Haïtien.note: Cap-Haïtien is the easiest and safest-feeling base in the north; do the Milot UNESCO circuit with a driver-guide, and start the Citadelle climb early before heat and clouds build.

days.theme: Boulevard du Cap, Cathedral & Market Streets

activities.name: Boulevard du Cap-Haïtien waterfront walk
Notre Dame Cathedral and Place d’Armes stop
Marché Cluny browse with driver standby
Héros de Vertières monument visit

days.theme: Milot UNESCO Day: Sans-Souci Palace & Citadelle Laferrière

activities.name: Sans-Souci Palace admission (usually bundled with National History Park ticket)
activities.cost: Included in ₹840 pp combined park ticket

activities.name: Citadelle Laferrière admission
Horse ride up the Citadelle approach with handler
Private guide for the Milot historical circuit

days.theme: Cormier Plage, Labadee Coast & Sunset Seafood

activities.name: Cormier Plage day pass or beach time
Labadee coastal boat or beach excursion
Cap-Haïtien gingerbread-era architecture walk
Sunset drinks on the boulevard
Relaxed beach morning or weather backup for the Citadelle circuit
Shopping for Haitian coffee, rum, or handicrafts
Moto-taxi and taxi hops around central Cap-Haïtien

Jacmel.name: Jacmel
Jacmel.note: Jacmel is compact and easiest in daylight; power cuts are normal, and Bassin Bleu is best done with a local guide plus proper shoes because the last section is steep and slippery.

days.theme: Historic Jacmel, Art Stops & Raymond les Bains

activities.name: Jacmel historic center and boardwalk walk
Artist studios or paper-mâché workshop visit
Raymond les Bains beach stop
Evening live-music or rum stop near the waterfront
Bassin Bleu entry + mandatory local guide
Moto-taxi or taxi transfer from Jacmel town to the trailhead
Rope-assisted hike to Bassin Clair and swim time
Post-hike beach lunch at Cyvadier

days.theme: Marigot or Slow Beach Buffer Day

activities.name: Marigot coastal drive or nearby beach-hopping
Souvenir shopping for masks and crafts
Relaxed coffee and gallery stop back in town

Île-à-Vache.name: Île-à-Vache
Île-à-Vache.note: Boat timing, fuel, and sea state matter more than distance here, so confirm your Les Cayes crossing the evening before and carry cash because island card acceptance is very limited.

activities.name: Boat transfer from Les Cayes / Madame Bernard dock to Île-à-Vache
Abaka Bay beach day
Village walk and local fishing-boat photo stops
Kayak or paddleboard rental through your hotel

days.theme: Island Boat Loop, Port Morgan & Snorkeling

activities.name: Private or shared boat loop to smaller coves and sand stretches
Snorkeling gear or guided reef swim
Port Morgan peninsula stop
Seafood sunset dinner on the island

days.theme: Weather Buffer or Full Slow-Island Day

activities.name: Extra beach time, hammock day, and village wandering
Motorbike taxi or walking transfer between island hamlets
Chartered boat back to mainland on your preferred schedule

connections.method: Sunrise Airways domestic flight (about 35–40m) is the practical choice; vetted private taxi or bus alternatives take most of a day and should only be attempted with up-to-date security advice.
Usually route back through Port-au-Prince by Sunrise Airways plus pre-booked taxi, or use a full-day private overland transfer only with a trusted operator.
Pre-booked taxi or shared tap-tap/minibus to Les Cayes, then a local boat from the Madame Bernard dock or nearby departure points to the island.

extras: Re-check your government travel advisory before booking and again right before departure; hotel closures, flight suspensions, and district-level restrictions can change faster than the weather forecast.
Tap-taps are the cheapest way around short routes, but most visitors still pair them with vetted taxis or hotel drivers for airport transfers and any movement after dark.
Carry small HTG notes for museum tickets, guides, moto-taxis, and island boats, because card readers and stable internet are unreliable outside major hotels.
Sunrise Airways domestic schedules can change around weather, security, or aircraft rotation, so avoid same-day international connections whenever possible.
For Bassin Bleu, wear grippy shoes and waterproof your phone; for the Citadelle, start early and budget extra time if you plan to ride partway up by horse.
